signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.35; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) vs BoJ 0.75% (ON HOLD) — 375bp carry differential favors USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7156; 25d RR at −0.28: mild put skew. Late-trend caution signal — watch for LF delta turning negative or volatility to decrease.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry premium/discount. GBP LF net long +28,426 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14) — constructive positioning. GBP/USD at 1.3505; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. ATM IV 9.5% near HV — options neutral.

signals.title: USD/CAD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoC at 2.25% vs Fed at 3.75% — 150bp CAD carry disadvantage. USD/CAD at 1.3658, testing the 50-day moving average. Positioning neutral; next key level at 1.3700. Implication: range-bound with a slight USD bias.
